Driving Schools Bond

Fast Approval & Lowest Rates

Get Your Driving School Bond Today

Fast approvals. Lowest rates. Guaranteed state licensing compliance. Licensed nationwide. A‑rated surety partners.

Guaranteed state compliance — your bond is issued to meet all driving school or traffic‑safety program regulations.

Trusted protection for students, instructors, and state regulators — ensuring every driving school operates ethically and legally.

Need help with a license or permit bond? Explore our License & Permit Bond Hub for state‑specific requirements and application guides.

![](/assets/images/Bond-Motor vehicle.png)

What Is a Driving School Bond?

A driving school bond (also called a driver training school bond or driving instructor bond) is a state‑required surety bond that guarantees driving schools operate ethically, follow licensing laws, and protect students from financial harm.

This bond protects the public from:

Misrepresentation of training services

Failure to provide paid instruction

Improper handling of tuition or fees

Violations of state driver‑education laws

Fraud or misconduct by instructors or staff

If you’re applying for or renewing a driving school license, your state may require this bond before granting approval.

Driving School Bond Amounts and Cost

PBond amounts vary by state and typically range from $5,000 to $50,000, depending on:

State regulations

Number of instructors

Type of training offered (classroom, behind‑the‑wheel, CDL, etc.)

Your premium depends on:

Bond amount

Credit score

Business financials

Licensing history

Most applicants qualify instantly with competitive rates.

Fast digital delivery — your driving school bond is ready for immediate filing with your licensing authority.

Who Needs a Driving School Bond?

You may need a driving school bond if you operate:

A teen driver‑education school

An adult driving school

A commercial driver training program (CDL)

A behind‑the‑wheel instruction service

A defensive driving or traffic‑school program

A private driver‑training business

Any business providing paid driver‑education services may be required to file this bond.

Driving School Bond Requirements & Eligibility

States typically require:

Completed driving school license application

State‑mandated bond amount

Credit review

Background check

Proof of curriculum and instructor qualifications

Annual or biannual renewal

Some states require separate bonds for branch locations or CDL training programs.

How the Driving School Bond Process Works

1

Complete the Application

Submit the quick, 60‑second application.

Fast nationwide approval — we match your bond to your state’s driving school licensing requirements.

2

Secure the Lowest Rate

We match you with the best rate available from A‑rated sureties.

3

Receive your bond

Same‑day issuance available for state filing.
